# Title: About Brand Registry facial data verification

Title: About Brand Registry facial data verification

URL Source: https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GSJL26WNCBGHTGL4

Markdown Content:
When you are enrolled in or use Amazon’s Brand Registry service, and at other times during the use of Amazon services, we verify your identity to protect our customers, selling partners, and our store. In some cases, we may offer you the option to complete identity verification by using facial recognition technology in the manner that is described on this page.

## Collected data

With your consent, we collect images of your face and government-issued identity documents from your mobile device’s camera. We refer to the unique facial measurements that we extract from these images as "facial data." Facial data may be considered "biometric data" under the laws of some jurisdictions.

## How we collect facial data

## How we collect facial data

We may directly collect facial data by using the Amazon Rekognition service and other technologies, or let one of our authorized third-party service providers, which includes Trulioo Information Services Inc. or its affiliates, to collect facial data using their own technology.

## How facial data is used

We use facial data to verify your identity and to prevent and detect fraud, such as by determining whether you are a live person and whether your face appears to match the picture on your government-issued identification documents. Facial data is only used for the purposes described, and it will not be used to develop or improve the quality of other technologies, products, or services.

## How long does Amazon keep facial data?

Amazon does not retain facial data generated through Brand Registry identity verification. The images that are used to create your facial data may be retained for longer periods to comply with legal requirements.

## How long do the service providers keep facial data?

Our service provider doesn't retain your facial data. The images that are used to create your facial data may be retained by our service provider for up to 90 days for audit purposes.

## How we protect your facial data

We take your data security very seriously. To protect your facial data, Amazon and its third-party service providers employ security measures that are at least as protective as the ones that are used to protect other confidential and sensitive data.

Your facial data and the images that are used to create your facial data are stored securely, are only accessed by authorized employees, and are only used for the purposes that are described herein.

## Is facial data sold to or shared with third parties?

We don’t sell or share your facial data, or the images that are used to create your facial data, with any third party unless it is required by applicable law.

We may authorize our third-party service providers, including Trulioo Information Services Inc. or its affiliates, to collect and process your facial data on our behalf by using their own technology. These third-party service providers are permitted to process and use your facial data only for the purpose of providing identity verification services to us, and only as described on this page and in a manner that is consistent with our [Privacy Notice](https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=201909010). They are not permitted to sell your facial data or share it with anyone besides Amazon.

## How do I withdraw consent for Amazon to use my facial data?

## How do I withdraw consent for Amazon to use my facial data?

Identity verification using facial recognition technology (biometrics) is optional. If you have already consented to have your facial data captured but have not yet uploaded your documents, you can exit out of the verification experience before any biometric data is created. Then, you'll need to [contact us](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/cu/contact-us) to enable identity verification with a video call.

## How can I request access to my facial data?

You can find information regarding how you can request access to your personal data in the Amazon [Privacy Notice](https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=201909010).
